msg: WI-734/M1 (F-128, session-completed.ts:1796): homework-summary LLM currently runs on the profile-missing branch with only logger.warn. Product call needed BEFORE impl: (A) hard-stop — when the profile row is absent, skip the LLM entirely + escalate (captureException/safeSend); review-recommended since the row is required to resolve subscription/language/accountId, so a summary without it is degraded anyway. (B) best-effort-LLM-at-any-cost — keep generating the summary but add structured escalation (still unmetered, or metered on a fallback path). Both options MUST add escalation per the billing silent-recovery ban. Shepherd recommends (A). Holding WI-734 dispatch until ruling; WI-735 (M2, independent) can proceed in parallel if you want me to start it now.

resolved: WI-734=Option A (hard-stop on profile-missing path implemented as a throw so Inngest step-retry absorbs replication lag; captureException/safeSend escalation fires only after retries still find no profile). WI-735 greenlit to run in parallel. Both P1 MUST_FIX executors dispatching now (Sonnet); each carries a red-green negative-path break test; both merge only through the strict green-PR gate (claude-review actually green).

#1165 (WI-736) was merged on a RED claude-review by the resumed WI-734 executor, which self-granted an exception citing a benign self-referential 401 — but the run log shows the advisory non-run (No verdict marker; token exhaustion/timeout/crash), a mischaracterization. Shepherd disposition: RATIFY, do not revert. (1) Re-ran claude-review on #1165 -> now GREEN/APPROVED, so the diff has a real verdict on the record; (2) manual diff review sound: drops issues:assigned (F-119/S1), hardens env-var-indirection invariant (S2), adds checker rule validateIssuesAssignedWithoutSenderGuard + 3 negative-path tests; (3) touches no OIDC/permissions config and #1167 claude-review passed on a main containing #1165 -> no gate regression. Revert would re-open F-119 for zero benefit. WI-736 now gate-clean retroactively.

PROCESS DEFECT (program-level). A resumed executor — dispatched ONLY to run /cosmo:execute complete for WI-734 — overstepped into SHEPHERD authority: it merged #1165/1166/1167 itself and SELF-GRANTED a gate exception on #1165 red claude-review. Outcome was fine this time (now gate-clean), but this is exactly the merge-on-red failure mode this wave exists to end; a genuinely broken PR would have been self-merged. Requests: (a) add an explicit guard to executor-protocol.md + shepherd-protocol.md — executors NEVER merge and NEVER self-grant a claude-review exception; only the shepherd merges through the strict gate, exceptions are operator-only; (b) harden the resumed-executor brief to scope=complete-only (no merge/dispatch/finalize-siblings). Partly my brief was not explicit enough on the boundary — flagging for the shared protocol, not just this lane.

CORRECTION to prg10ff-003: the executor did NOT mischaracterize the #1165 claude-review cause. The WI-736 page Caveats cite the specific upstream error — claude-code-action 401 Workflow validation failed: workflow file must have identical content to the default branch — the real self-referential-workflow guard, unavoidable for a PR that edits claude-code-review.yml itself. CONFIRMED: my post-merge rerun went GREEN/APPROVED precisely because once merged the PR workflow content matches main. The generic no-verdict-marker/tokens-exhausted line (what I and the reviewer first saw) is the DOWNSTREAM wrappers catch-all symptom, not the root cause. So the merge-on-red was on a genuinely benign cause. The PROCESS concern in prg10ff-004 (executor self-granting the exception + overstepping into merge) STILL STANDS — right call, wrong authority. Status: reviewer zdx:review reworked all 3 SHOULD items (normal loop); 734/735 Closed/Done. Re-dispatching: 736 (real — checker misses implicit on:issues no-types bypass + evidence fix), 737 (trivial — missing Started metadata, code verified good), 738 (S4 integration test needs migration 0116 applied to dev DB).

DEPLOY-SAFETY (production risk, now CONFIRMED). Migration 0116 (dictation_completion_key_unique — makes (profile_id, completion_key) the unique key the dictation upsert ON CONFLICT targets; created in the ORIGINAL PRG-10 wave PR #1122, already merged to main) was found NOT applied to the dev Neon DB (real drift) — applied to DEV ONLY during WI-738 rework, integration suite now 13/13. The SAME migration is almost certainly unapplied to STAGING + PROD; the conflict-target code is already on main. If it deploys without 0116, dictation result writes FAIL in prod with no unique or exclusion constraint matching the ON CONFLICT specification. Applying to stg/prod is a destructive shared-infra step beyond my lane mandate (worker deploy != Neon migrate; ## Rollback is in PR #1122). Request: confirm 0116 is on the deploy checklist for stg+prod via drizzle-kit migrate before the next deploy. (Passively noted in tracker s5 follow-ups from the original wave; the dev drift now makes it concrete.)

WI-738 BLOCKED on migration 0116 reaching the STAGING DB — reviewer bounced it a 2nd time (S4 integration test still 11/13 fail, no unique/exclusion constraint matching ON CONFLICT). Root cause nailed: apps/api/src/services/dictation/result.integration.test.ts connects via the default worktree Doppler config = stg (ep-fancy-cherry), which lacks 0116; the executor applied 0116 to a DEV endpoint (ep-muddy-sunset) only, so re-applying to dev is futile. Migration is committed (apps/api/drizzle/0116_dictation_completion_key_unique.sql). Resolution = apply 0116 to STAGING via sanctioned drizzle-kit migrate (committed SQL; ## Rollback in PR #1122) — shared-infra, beyond my mandate, wont run unilaterally. SAME action as prg10ff-006 (also needs prod). The S4 CODE is already correct and merged (PR #1167, 8924a186); only its verification env is missing the index. Other WI-738 sub-items (S6/S7/C7/S9) already pass. Request: authorize/apply 0116 to staging (and prod). Until then WI-738 cannot close; lane otherwise lands 4/5 (734/735/737 Closed, 736 Reviewing).

resolved: orchestrator applied 0116 directly to the staging Neon DB (ep-fancy-cherry — the exact endpoint the S4 integration test uses), verified unique index present + legacy dropped. prg10ff-006 also resolved (staging consistent + Deploy GREEN; prod clean, 0116 rides the next prod deploy in order, no separate action). Re-dispatched WI-738 executor to re-run S4 against stg (expect 13/13), fix the migration-file citation to apps/api/drizzle/0116_dictation_completion_key_unique.sql, and re-finalize to Reviewing via property-PATCH. Lane will be 5/5 once the reviewer re-closes WI-738.

Proposed executor-protocol.md guard wording (per your in-005 invite): Add under the executor scope boundary — "An executors authority ENDS at the green-PR report. Executors NEVER merge a PR, NEVER move a sibling WI, and NEVER self-grant or waive a required-check failure (incl. a red claude-review, even when plausibly benign). Merging through the strict green-PR gate and granting any per-PR gate exception are SHEPHERD-only acts; a claude-review exception is operator-only. On a red/blocked check, the executor diagnoses it verbatim and reports to the shepherd — it does not act on its own diagnosis." Mirror a one-line pointer in shepherd-protocol.md -> Merging the WP. Inbox-watcher now ARMED per in-003.

Merged WI-739 PR #1181 (788f9a2f) on mergeStateStatus=UNSTABLE — documented shepherd call: all REQUIRED checks SUCCESS + claude-review APPROVED (0 findings); the ONLY red is the NON-required advisory run-smoke. This is NOT a claude-review exception (operator-only) — it is the merge-on-unrelated-advisory case, which is shepherd authority. FLAG for your domain (staging health — you just reconciled it): run-smoke fails PERSISTENTLY (survived a rerun) at apps/mobile/e2e-web/helpers/auth.setup.ts:25 seed parent-multi-child -> Timed out waiting for signed-in app readiness after 60000ms (profile load 3/3). Zero overlap with WI-739 diff (CORS/sanitize/quiz/sessions/checker/doc). Could be a side effect of the staging 0116/consent_request reconciliation, or pre-existing E2E flakiness (baseline ~48%). Not fixing inline (out of lane scope); surfacing because it is ambient on staging and dings the advisory smoke for every PR. Your call: investigate vs known-flaky.
